source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute
source.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.
source.setting: Hospital and emergency pediatric assessment; hosted by the Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne and PIC endorsed.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute

source.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute
source.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.
source.setting: Hospital and emergency pediatric assessment; hosted by the Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne and PIC endorsed.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.

rewritten_legacy_items.final_wording: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.

added_items.final_wording: Defines acute pediatric abdominal pain as a common nonspecific presentation requiring assessment for urgent causes and red flags.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute
source.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.
source.setting: Hospital and emergency pediatric assessment; hosted by the Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne and PIC endorsed.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines acute pediatric abdominal pain as a common nonspecific presentation requiring assessment for urgent causes and red flags.

added_items.rationale: Directly supports acute pediatric abdominal-pain presentation terminology and the need to assess for urgent causes.

added_items.final_wording: Specifies pain characteristics, systemic symptoms, diet, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent, psychosocial, and infant presentation history.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute
source.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.
source.setting: Hospital and emergency pediatric assessment; hosted by the Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne and PIC endorsed.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies pain characteristics, systemic symptoms, diet, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent, psychosocial, and infant presentation history.

added_items.rationale: Directly supports mapped pain, systemic, stool, vomiting, urinary, diet, and infant-history domains.

added_items.final_wording: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute
source.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.
source.setting: Hospital and emergency pediatric assessment; hosted by the Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne and PIC endorsed.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.

added_items.rationale: Directly supports mapped appearance, vital signs, hydration, abdominal examination, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, and distension documentation.

added_items.final_wording: States that most children need no investigations and lists targeted urine, blood, and imaging investigations selected by the likely cause.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute
source.population: Children and adolescents with acute abdominal pain.
source.setting: Hospital and emergency pediatric assessment; hosted by the Royal Children’s Hospital Melbourne and PIC endorsed.
source.exclusions: Exact for acute pediatric abdominal pain but partial for general outpatient use, chronic pain, and UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: States that most children need no investigations and lists targeted urine, blood, and imaging investigations selected by the likely cause.

added_items.rationale: Directly supports mapped urinalysis and clinician-selected investigation documentation while noting that most children need none.

added_items.final_wording: Lists severe pain, peritonism, bilious vomiting, suspected surgical disease, admission, follow-up, and parent advice contexts.
